titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/204

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=439 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:22:05.375858+00 | translation moa=440 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:22:03.776274+00

p.cl: Sciant omnes tam presentes quam futuri quod

Ego Iohannes Auenel filius Geruasii Auenel dedi . concessi . et hac presenti carta mea confirmaui . deo et sancte Marie . et porte
de Melros . ad usus pauperum ibidem aduenientium . pro salute anime mee . et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum in puram et pereptuam elemosinam . illam dimidiam
carucatam terre in territorio de Torthorald . quam Willelmus filius Glay . mihi pro homagio et seruicio meo dedit . Tenendam et habendam predicte porte de Melros in
perpetuum . adeo libere . quiete et honorifice . In bosco . in plano . in pratis et pascuis . in viis et semitis . in stagnis et molendinis . in moris et marisis . in aquis et omnibus
libertatibus et aisiamentis ad illam terram pertinentibus . sicut aliqua elemosina liberius . quiecius . et honorificencius in toto regno Scotie tenetur aut possidetur .
Reddendo inde annuatim domino feodi pro omni seruicio . consuetudine . exaccione et demanda . vnam libram piperis ad natale domini . et faciendo forinsecum seruicium Domini
Regis . quantum pertinet ad dimidiam carucatam terre . Ego uero et heredes mei supradictam dimidiam carucatam terre supradicte porte de melros cum omnibus pertinenciis
suis ac libertatibus contra omnes homines et feminas in perpetuum warentizabimus . manutenebimus ac defendemus .
In cuius rei testimonium presenti scripto sigillum meum feci apponi .

Hiis Test’ . Domino Ricardo Cumyn . Domino Thoma de Normanilla . Domino Aymero de Makeswele . Domino Thoma filio . Ranulfi . Domino Thoma de chartris .
Helya de Dundas . Domino Hugone . Mauleuerer . Roberto filio helye . Roberto Poydras . Et multis aliis .
Let all men as well present as future know that

I, John Avenel, son of Gervase Avenel, have given, granted, and by this my present charter have made firm, to God and Saint Mary, and the gate of Melrose, for the benefit of the poor who come there, for the salvation of my soul, and of all my ancestors and successors, in pure and perpetual alms, the half-ploughgate of land in the territory of Torthorwald, which William son of Glay gave me in return for my homage and service; for the aforesaid gate of Melrose to have and to hold for ever, as freely, quitely and honourably, in wood, in plain, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in pools and mills, in moors and marshes, in waters and all liberties and easements that belong to that land, just as any alms is freely, quitely, and honourably held and possessed in the whole rekingdom of Scotland; by returning from it to the lord of the feu for every service, custom, exaction and demand, one pound of pepper and the Lord's Nativity, and performing the forinsec service of the lord king, as much as belongs to half a ploughgate of land. And I and my heirs shall warrant, maintain and defend the above-said half ploughgate of land to the above-said gate of Melrose, with all its pertinents and liberties, against all men and women for ever.
In testimony of this matter, I have cause my seal to be fixed to the present writing.

With these witnesses: the lord Richard Comyn, the lord Thomas de Normanville, the lord Eymer of Maxwell, the lord Thomas son of Ranulf, the lord Thomas de Chartres, Elias of Dundas, the lord Hugh Mauleverer, Robert son of Elias, Robert Poydras, and many others.
